paper_metadata.title: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models
paper_metadata.abstract: We investigate the optimal model size and number of tokens for training a transformer language model under a given compute budget. We find that current large language models are significantly undertrained, a consequence of the recent focus on scaling language models whilst keeping the amount of training data constant. By training over 400 language models ranging from 70 million to over 16 billion parameters on 5 to 500 billion tokens, we find that for compute-optimal training, the model size and the number of training tokens should be scaled equally: for every doubling of model size the number of training tokens should also be doubled. We test this hypothesis by training a predicted compute-optimal model, Chinchilla, that uses the same compute budget as Gopher but with 70B parameters and 4$\times$ more more data. Chinchilla uniformly and significantly outperforms Gopher (280B), GPT-3 (175B), Jurassic-1 (178B), and Megatron-Turing NLG (530B) on a large range of downstream evaluation tasks. This also means that Chinchilla uses substantially less compute for fine-tuning and inference, greatly facilitating downstream usage. As a highlight, Chinchilla reaches a state-of-the-art average accuracy of 67.5% on the MMLU benchmark, greater than a 7% improvement over Gopher.

extraction_data.innovation: Chinchilla is a compute-optimized large language model that achieves state-of-the-art performance on various benchmarks (MMLU, RACE, LAMBADA, etc.) with significantly fewer parameters than Gopher, demonstrating that better data modeling and training efficiency can lead to better performance.
extraction_data.extension: Chinchilla is designed to optimize compute usage by training on more data with a smaller model size, achieving better performance than larger models like Gopher.
extraction_data.license: Not specified (likely closed source as per DeepMind's typical practices)
extraction_data.application: General language tasks, including but not limited to language understanding, question answering, and text generation.

extraction_data.innovation: Optimized training of a 1 trillion parameter model using a large, diverse dataset and a cosine learning rate schedule to achieve state-of-the-art performance with minimal computational resources.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: MassiveText (including subsets: MassiveWeb, Books, C4, News, GitHub, Wikipedia)
extraction_data.application: Language understanding, text generation, and other NLP tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient training of large language models
extraction_data.optimizer: Cosine learning rate schedule with 10× decay

extraction_data.innovation: Scaling a large language model to 1e21 parameters using optimized training methods to achieve state-of-the-art performance with minimal computational resources.
